brand.name: Anamata Kāhui
brand.tagline: A collective platform unifying records, research, creative arts, and technology for Aotearoa.

home.lede: Anamata Kāhui is the collective platform unifying Anamata Records, Research & Language Preservation, Creative Arts, and Technology & Development. Built for artists, researchers, and clients who want everything in one place — and nothing held back.
home.branchesLede: Each branch has its own team, tooling, and dashboard — but they all share the same auth, the same data model, and the same cultural foundation.
home.closerTitle: Built on Supabase + Next.js. Deployed on Vercel.
home.closerLede: Row-level security scopes every record to its owner. Branch membership controls the dashboards you see. Public routes stay fast with edge caching.

records.name: Anamata Records
records.tagline: Music branch — artist portal, release pipeline, stem vault, and royalty analytics.

artistPortal.title: Artist portal
artistPortal.body: Each artist manages their roster, releases, and royalties in one place.

releasePipeline.title: Release pipeline
releasePipeline.body: Track a release from draft through scheduled drop to post-release analytics.

stemVault.title: Stem vault
stemVault.body: Secure, versioned storage for masters, stems, and supporting artwork.

streamRoyalty.title: Stream & royalty
streamRoyalty.body: Performance metrics from Spotify, Apple Music, and the local distributors.

research.name: Research & Language Preservation
research.tagline: Knowledge vault, document archives, and field projects for language preservation.

documentVault.title: Document vault
documentVault.body: Searchable archive of primary sources, transcripts, and field notes.

fieldProjects.title: Field projects
fieldProjects.body: Live tracker for in-progress fieldwork with iwi-gate and consent metadata.

languageTools.title: Language tools
languageTools.body: Reo Māori corpus utilities — concordance, frequency, and dialect markers.

publications.title: Publications
publications.body: Peer-reviewed and community publications with DOI minting.

arts.name: Creative Arts
arts.tagline: Visual arts, digital media showcase, and cultural design portfolios.

galleries.title: Galleries
galleries.body: Curated collections of visual work — high-resolution, slow-loaded, captioned.

digitalMedia.title: Digital media
digitalMedia.body: Generative, motion, and interactive pieces served from edge-cached storage.

portfolios.title: Portfolios
portfolios.body: Each artist or designer has a portfolio with provenance and exhibition history.

commissions.title: Commissions
commissions.body: Brief intake and project tracking for commissioned work.

dev.name: Technology & Development
dev.tagline: Software projects, client tools, and internal automation for the Kāhui.

clientTools.title: Client tools
clientTools.body: Dashboards and utilities built for the Kāhui's partners.

openSource.title: Open source
openSource.body: Reusable libraries and starters released under permissive licences.

automation.title: Automation
automation.body: Internal scripts and pipelines for catalog, release, and analytics work.

apiConsole.title: API console
apiConsole.body: Authenticated route to manage API keys and integration secrets.

about.title: About Anamata Kāhui
about.lede: Anamata Kāhui is the digital ecosystem that holds Anamata Records, Research & Language Preservation, Creative Arts, and Technology & Development under one roof. Each branch has its own team, dashboards, and tools — but they share the same auth, the same data model, and the same cultural foundation.

tino.title: Tino rangatiratanga
tino.body: Self-determination at every layer — the platform serves the people who build on it, not the other way around.

kaitiaki.title: Kaitiakitanga
kaitiaki.body: Cultural data is governed by the communities it comes from. RLS, consent metadata, and audit trails are first-class.

whanaunga.title: Whanaungatanga
whanaunga.body: The four branches share an identity, an auth system, and a code of conduct. They don't exist in isolation.

about.getInvolvedLede: Whether you're an artist, researcher, designer, or engineer — there's a place in the Kāhui.

contact.title: Contact
contact.lede: Drop us a line. We reply within a few working days.
contact.cardLede: For licensing, partnerships, or general enquiries.
contact.name: Name

footer.tagline: A collective platform unifying records, research, creative arts, and technology for Aotearoa.

login.title: Sign in
login.lede: Access the artist portal, dashboards, and the Kāhui console.

register.title: Join the Kāhui
register.lede: Create an account to access the artist portal and dashboards.

reset.title: Reset password
reset.lede: We'll send a recovery link to your email.

cookieConsent.title: Cookies on this site
cookieConsent.body: We use strictly-necessary cookies to keep the site working and optional analytics cookies to understand how it's used. You can accept all, reject non-essential, or customise.

necessary.label: Strictly necessary
necessary.description: Required for the site to work. Always on.

analytics.label: Analytics
analytics.description: Help us understand how the site is used. Off by default.

preferences.label: Preferences
preferences.description: Remember your language and accessibility settings.

privacyNotice.title: Privacy notice
privacyNotice.summary: This notice explains how Anamata Kāhui collects, holds, uses, and discloses your personal information under the NZ Privacy Act 2020. We also honour the CARE principles of Te Mana Raraunga Māori Data Sovereignty. The full document below is in English; a te reo Māori summary follows the table of contents.
privacyNotice.tocHow: How we hold and protect it

cookiePolicy.title: Cookie policy
cookiePolicy.summary: This policy lists every cookie set by anamatakahui.co.nz, what it does, how long it lives, and how you can control it. The full English policy is below; a te reo Māori summary explains the key points.

terms.title: Terms of use
terms.summary: These terms govern use of anamatakahui.co.nz and the four branches. By using the site you accept these terms. The full English terms are below; a te reo Māori summary explains the key points.

anonymous.title: Privacy controls
anonymous.lede: Sign in to view your data, request export, request deletion, or change your public directory listing.

dataExport.title: Export your data
dataExport.description: Download a copy of your account data — profile, public directory listing, contact enquiries, and consent log entries.

dataDeletion.title: Request account deletion
dataDeletion.description: Submit a request for your account to be deleted. We'll confirm by email before any deletion takes effect.

directory.title: Public directory listing
directory.description: Your profile may appear in the public Kāhui directory (for artists, researchers, designers). Toggle off to remove yourself.

cookies.title: Cookie preferences
cookies.description: Update your consent for analytics and preferences cookies.

notFound.title: Page not found
notFound.description: The page you were looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.

generic.title: Something went wrong
generic.description: An unexpected error occurred. We've been notified. Please try again.

research.title: Research & Language Preservation
research.lede: Reo Māori research, field projects, and accessible scholarship — published openly.

forFunders.title: For funders
forFunders.lede: For people and organisations deciding whether to fund Anamata Kāhui. Live evidence, active funding rounds, and the cultural-governance posture that de-risks investment.

waiata.title: Anamata Records
waiata.lede: The music branch — released waiata, roster, stem vault, and analytics.

kaikorero.title: Kaikōrero profile
kaikorero.subtitle: Your cultural-knowledge surface
kaikorero.lede: Declare what you carry knowledge of, and which iwi or region that knowledge is rooted in. Other creators across iwi can then find you for collaboration. You stay in control of what's public.
kaikorero.directoryLede: Members of the kāhui who carry cultural knowledge across iwi. Discovery works by filter — find someone who carries the pūrākau, reo, or tikanga you're looking for, scoped to the iwi or region where they hold standing.

sections.bioHelp: The fuller story you'd put on a public profile. Plain text; shown on your Kaikōrero page if you're public.
sections.iwiHelp: Self-declared. Separates from any attested set the platform promotes you into later (kaitiaki roster membership, or endorsement by an attested member).
sections.knowledgeAreasHelp: What you carry knowledge of. Each area can be scoped to a specific iwi or region (e.g. pūrākau of Ngāti Porou). Scoped knowledge is the discovery primitive — people filter by what they need, not by who you are.
sections.visibilityHelp: Both settings are opt-in. Public visibility requires the public-directory opt-in to also be enabled.

fields.bioPlaceholder: Tell other creators what you carry knowledge of, where you're rooted, and what you're hoping to collaborate on.
fields.bioHint: Up to 2000 characters. The existing profile bio (separate field) is unaffected.
fields.iwiHint: Claimed iwi are surfaced on your profile with a "claimed" tag. Attested iwi (added by kaitiaki or after platform activity) are shown as plain affiliations.
fields.duplicateHint: You can add the same domain multiple times if it carries different iwi or regional scope.
fields.visibilityPublic: List me in the public Kaikōrero directory
fields.visibilityPublicHelp: Requires the public-directory opt-in on privacy controls to also be enabled.
fields.availableForTonoHelp: Receive help-request invitations from other creators. You can turn this off temporarily without hiding your profile.
fields.emptyAreas: No knowledge areas yet — add one below.

kaikorero.publicNotVisible: Not currently public. To appear in the Kaikōrero directory you need both this profile visible and the public directory opt-in enabled.

directory.filterHelp: Showing kaikōrero whose declared knowledge areas intersect the filter. Discovery is by filter — no aggregate counts, no ranking.
directory.noResultsFiltered: No kaikōrero match these filters. Try widening the iwi search or removing the domain filter.
directory.noResultsEmpty: No public-directory profiles yet. Members can opt in via the dashboard Kaikōrero profile editor.

profilePage.collaborateBody: Members of the kāhui can post a tono (help request) scoped to the cultural area they need help with. The platform surfaces this kaikōrero as a candidate — collaboration happens off-platform or in the dashboard, never via direct message.
profilePage.collaborateNote: Tono board ships with Phase 3 (this release: profile & discovery).

messages.savedPublic: Profile saved. You are listed in the public Kaikōrero directory.
messages.savedPrivate: Profile saved. Hidden from the public directory.
messages.bioTooLong: Bio must be 2000 characters or fewer.
messages.parseFailed: Knowledge areas payload could not be parsed. Please try again.
messages.clearFailed: Could not clear existing knowledge areas: {message}

errors.profileNotFoundDesc: Your profile row is missing. Contact an admin to onboard you.

roster.title: My roster
roster.lede: View your status on every branch where you're a member of the roster. This page is read-only — only admins can change your status or visibility.
roster.emptyTitle: You're not on any branch's roster yet
roster.emptyBody: If you'd like to be added to a branch's roster, talk to a branch admin. They'll need your details to set things up — and you'll both sign off on the relationship before any release goes through.
roster.infoBody: The roster is the list of artists a branch represents. Being on a roster means you have a formal relationship with the branch — for music that's Anamata Records, for research that means your work is associated with the research division. Each row on this page is one (artist, branch) pairing.
roster.signLede: By signing, you confirm you have read and agree to the terms of this contract. The contract body is frozen — what you sign is what you get.
roster.confirmSign: Confirm — I have read and agree

policies.acknowledgeLede: By acknowledging, you confirm you have read and agree to the terms of this policy. The policy body is frozen — what you sign is what you get.
policies.notesPlaceholder: e.g. I noted concern about clause 3 but agree
policies.notesHint: Your notes are visible to admins.
policies.confirmAcknowledge: Confirm — I have read and agree
policies.withdrawLede: Your original acknowledgement remains in the audit trail. If you withdraw, your note will be recorded but the platform no longer treats you as covered by this policy.
policies.withdrawReasonHint: Used to follow up if the policy changes.

demos.title: My demos
demos.lede: Demos are work-in-progress uploads that haven't reached the release pipeline. Submit a demo for cultural review before it can be promoted to a full release.
demos.emptyTitle: You're not on any branch's roster
demos.emptyBody: Talk to a branch admin to be added to the roster before you upload demos.
demos.uploadLede: Fill in the metadata. Your file gets uploaded to storage before you submit this form.
demos.submitConfirmLede: This demo becomes visible to kaitiaki for review. You'll be notified when it's approved or rejected.
demos.approveConfirmLede: After approval, a branch admin can promote this demo to a release.
demos.rejectConfirmLede: This is terminal. If you want a re-review, upload a new demo.
demos.promoteConfirmLede: A new release row will be created from this demo. The demo will be marked promoted.
demos.errorTooLarge: File is too large ({size}). Maximum for this type is {limit}.
demos.errorRateLimit: Too many uploads. Please wait a few minutes and try again.
demos.errorQuota: You've exceeded your storage or demo quota.
demos.errorDemoLimit: You've reached the demo limit. Delete some drafts or submit them for review to free up space.

endorsements.title: Endorsements
endorsements.subtitle: Contribution lineage
endorsements.lede: Endorsements are the credibility layer of the kāhui. Each one is scoped to a work, a domain, and (often) an iwi. They show up on your public kaikōrero profile so other creators across iwi can find you for collaboration.

given.empty: You haven't given any endorsements yet.
given.revokePlaceholder: Visible to the recipient and on your public profile. Be specific — this is part of the cultural lineage.

received.empty: You haven't received any endorsements yet. When a kaitiaki approves a release you collaborated on, you'll see an auto-endorsement here.

lineage.title: Contribution lineage
lineage.subtitle: Each endorsement below is scoped to a work and (where applicable) a specific knowledge domain and iwi. Specificity over aggregation — no "47 endorsements" rollup.

messages.revokeSuccess: Endorsement revoked. The recipient's profile now shows it with your reason.
messages.profileWorkConflict: Profile-anchored endorsements must not have a work_id.
messages.workRequired: Non-profile endorsements must reference a work.

errors.emptyDesc: Endorsements land here automatically when a kaitiaki approves a release you collaborated on. You can also give endorsements manually from a kaikōrero's public profile.

fields.workTypeHelp: Default 'Standing endorsement' is general support of the person's body of work. Pick a work-anchored mode to scope this endorsement to a specific release, lyric, or stem.
fields.notesPlaceholder: What does this person carry knowledge of? Why are you endorsing them? This becomes part of the public cultural lineage.
fields.notesHelp: Notes are visible to the recipient and on the public endorsement ribbon. Be specific.

give.publicNote: You've endorsed this kaikōrero. Manage your endorsements from your

board.title: Your help requests
board.subtitle: Tono board
board.lede: Post a tono when you need cross-iwi help with cultural knowledge — narrative verification, te reo review, mentorship, or a co-creator for a release. Other kaikōrero see your request and can propose.
board.noActiveDesc: Post one when you need cultural help with a work in progress.
board.openInboxLabel: Open tono you can help on

compose.title: Ask the kāhui for help
compose.lede: Post a help request scoped to a specific kind of cultural knowledge and (optionally) a specific iwi. Other kaikōrero who carry that knowledge can respond with proposals.
compose.requestItselfDesc: Be specific about what you need. Other kaikōrero read this to decide whether to propose.
compose.requestBodyPlaceholder: I'm working on a waiata that draws on the pūrākau of Ngātoroirangi and need someone from Taranaki to verify the place-name references. The track is in early mixing.
compose.kohaDesc: What you're offering in return. Could be co-credit, future reciprocity, a payment, or empty if the koha is the relationship itself. Be honest — the system doesn't enforce what you write here, but the cultural standing you accrue depends on it.
compose.kohaPlaceholder: e.g. co-credit on the waiata, future koha reciprocity, $300, or 'nothing — just the relationship'
compose.kohaMonetary: This is a monetary offer (so the UI can flag it)
compose.visibilityDesc: Who can see this tono. Open is the default; iwi-specific restricts visibility to kaikōrero with attested affiliation to the iwi you specify.
compose.anchorReleaseDesc: If this tono relates to a specific release, link it here. The tono will appear on that release's detail page on fulfillment.
compose.iwiSpecificHelp: You'll need to fill in scope_iwi above.

detail.pendingDesc: Waiting for the tono creator to respond.
detail.acceptedDesc: Accepted! The tono is in conversation. Wait for the creator to mark it fulfilled.
detail.declinedDesc: The tono creator declined your proposal.

proposal.title: Propose to help
proposal.lede: The tono creator will see your proposal and can accept or decline. If accepted, the tono moves to in conversation; when the work lands, mark it fulfilled to receive an endorsement.
proposal.yourProposalPlaceholder: Explain what you'd contribute and how. Be specific about scope and time.
proposal.declineReasonPlaceholder: Visible to the proposer. Keeps the cultural lineage honest.
proposal.endorsementNote: Your collaborator will be auto-endorsed as a co-creator on the linked release (if any). They'll be notified.
proposal.proposedKohaPlaceholder: What you'd accept in return. Reciprocity later, co-credit, a fixed amount, or nothing.

lifecycle.title: Lifecycle actions
lifecycle.description: Move the tono to its final state. Fulfill when the work lands; withdraw if the request is no longer relevant; close if you're not going to fulfil it.
lifecycle.closeWarning: Closing without fulfilling will decline any pending proposals. The accepted proposer will see this in their activity.
lifecycle.notePlaceholder: e.g. Released 'Te Tinihanga' on 2026-09-12, your guidance landed perfectly in the bridge.
lifecycle.withdrawReason: Why are you withdrawing? Open proposals will be declined with this reason.
lifecycle.closeReason: Why are you closing without fulfilling?

inbox.title: Help requests you can answer
inbox.lede: Open help requests from across the kāhui. Filtered by your attested iwi affiliation — iwi-specific tono only appear if your attested set includes the requested iwi.
inbox.emptyDesc: No open tono match your visibility. Check back later, or browse the Kaikōrero directory to find people to collaborate with directly.

visibility.openHelp: Any kaikōrero on the platform can see and propose.
visibility.invitedHelp: Only kaikōrero you specifically invite can see this.
visibility.iwiSpecificHelp: Only kaikōrero with attested affiliation to the iwi you name can see this.

messages.createdOpen: Tono posted and visible to the kāhui.
messages.createdIwi: Tono posted. Visible to kaikōrero with attested affiliation to this iwi.
messages.createdInvited: Tono posted. Add invitees to direct it.
messages.proposed: Proposal submitted. The tono creator will see it and can accept or decline.
messages.proposalAccepted: Proposal accepted. Tono is now in conversation. Mark it fulfilled when the work lands.
messages.proposalDeclined: Proposal declined. The proposer has been notified.
messages.fulfilled: Tono fulfilled. The proposer has been notified.
messages.fulfilledWithEndorsement: Tono fulfilled. Your collaborator has been endorsed on this work and notified.
messages.withdrawn: Tono withdrawn. Open proposals have been declined with the reason recorded.
messages.closed: Tono closed. Open proposals have been declined.
messages.kohaTooLong: Koha description must be 2000 characters or fewer.
messages.declineReasonTooLong: Decline reason must be 2000 characters or fewer.
messages.notCreator: Only the tono creator can respond to proposals.
messages.tonoNotAccepting: Tono is no longer accepting responses.
messages.proposalAcceptedButTonoFailed: Proposal accepted but tono status update failed: {message}. Please try again or contact an admin.
messages.selfPropose: You cannot propose on your own tono.
messages.alreadyProposed: You've already proposed on this tono. Withdraw your existing proposal first if you'd like to revise it.
messages.tonoNotFound: Tono not found or not visible to you.
messages.tonoNotAcceptingProposals: Tono is no longer accepting proposals.
messages.notCreatorLifecycle: Only the tono creator can mark it fulfilled.
messages.notCreatorWithdraw: Only the tono creator can withdraw.
messages.notCreatorClose: Only the tono creator can close.

empty.title: No notifications yet
empty.hint: Notifications appear here as activity happens.

inbox.title: All activity
inbox.subtitle: Notifications
inbox.lede: Endorsements you've received and tono activity across the kāhui.
inbox.emptyDescFilter: No {{kind}} notifications. Switch tabs to see other activity.
inbox.emptyDescAll: Endorsements and tono activity will show up here as they happen.

messages.markAllFailed: Could not mark all notifications read: {{message}}

prefs.title: Notification preferences
prefs.lede: Choose which activity you hear about
prefs.description: Toggle the channels for each kind. Defaults restore on Reset.
prefs.inAppDesc: Shows in the bell icon and inbox. Always free; no extra setup.
prefs.emailDesc: An email digest will go to your registered address. (Wired up when email fanout ships.)

kindDesc.endorsement_received: Someone endorsed you for work, knowledge, or standing.
kindDesc.endorsement_revoked: An endorsement you gave or received was revoked with a reason.
kindDesc.tono_proposal_received: Someone proposed on one of your open tono.
kindDesc.tono_proposal_accepted: A tono creator accepted your proposal — work can begin.
kindDesc.tono_proposal_declined: A tono creator declined your proposal.
kindDesc.tono_fulfilled: A tono you helped on was marked fulfilled by the creator.

prefs.culturalDesc: When a tono is fulfilled, an endorsement is created automatically. Notes from the endorser become part of the cultural lineage. Endorsements, once given, may be revoked with a reason — revocation doesn't delete the lineage, it adds a layer.

records.createLede: Onboard a new artist to this branch's roster. They start as 'prospect' by default.
records.onRosterPublicly: Show on the public roster list
records.onRosterPubliclyHint: Admits the artist's name to the public roster list.
records.optedInPublicHint: The artist has agreed to their whakapapa and details being shown publicly.
records.internalNotesPlaceholder: Notes for managers only — never shown to the artist.
records.noTransitionsAvailable: No transitions available from this status.
records.visibilityLede: Both flags must be true for the artist to be visible publicly.

contracts.createLede: Pick an artist, a document from the library, and the contract term. The document body is frozen at create time.
contracts.noRosterEntries: No active artists on this branch's roster. Add one first via the Roster page.

policies.title: Title
policies.lede: Platform legal policies. Create new ones from the document library; the body is frozen at create time. Publishing a new version automatically demotes the previous current.
policies.createLede: Pick the policy type, the document version from the library, and the effective date.
policies.noPolicies: No policies yet. Use the Create button to draft one from a document in the library.

publicRoster.title: Artists on the roster
publicRoster.lede: The signed artists across Anamata Kāhui's branches who have consented to public display.
publicRoster.noteBody: This list shows artists who have signed onto a branch's roster and consented to public visibility. We do not show artists who have not opted in, and we do not show counts, rankings, or aggregate statistics — just names and roles.
publicRoster.emptyTitle: No opted-in artists on this branch yet
publicRoster.emptyBody: If you'd like to be added, talk to a branch admin.
